claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start, I need to understand a bit about your situation.

**Do you have a child (or children) you're claiming for?** If yes, I'll need to know:

1. **How old is your child?** (or if you have multiple children, let's start with one and we can check each one)
2. **What is your child's name?** (so I can keep track if we're checking multiple children)

The eligibility rules depend on the child's age:
- **Under 16**: They may automatically qualify
- **16-19**: They may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- **20 or over**: Generally not eligible

Please tell me about your child or children, and we'll work through the eligibility together!

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes I'm interested in claiming Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old and lives at home with me. I wanted to check if I'm eligible to claim for him?

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for that information. Let me help you check eligibility for your son Alex, who is 8 years old.

Since Alex is under 16, that's a good start. Now I need to ask you an important question:

**Is Alex working full-time (meaning 24 hours or more per week)?**

This is very unlikely for an 8-year-old, but I need to confirm this as part of the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, not at all - Alex doesn't work. He's only 8, so he's just in school full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's perfectly fine and as expected! Alex is in school full-time and not working, which is good.

Now, the next question:

**Is Alex receiving any income-based benefits in his own name?**

Examples would include:
- Income Support
- income-based Jobseeker's Allowance
- income-related Employment and Support Allowance
- Universal Credit

Again, this is very unlikely for an 8-year-old child, but I need to confirm this as part of the eligibility check.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name. He's just a regular school-age child.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Alex doesn't receive any benefits in his own name, which is what we'd expect.

Next question:

**Is Alex in an apprenticeship or any similar arrangement?**

This would include things like:
- Advanced or higher apprenticeships
- Scottish Vocational Qualifications at level 3 or above
- Similar work-based training programs

Again, for an 8-year-old in regular school, the answer is almost certainly no, but I need to confirm this as part of the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not. Alex is just 8 years old and in primary school. He's not in any apprenticeship or training program like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! That's exactly what I expected. Alex is just in regular primary school, so he's not in any disqualifying apprenticeship.

Now we've confirmed that Alex meets all the child-related requirements. Next, I need to ask about **your residency status** as the claimant:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

Or more specifically, do any of the following apply to you:

1. You normally live in the UK
2. You normally live in a qualifying European country (EEA or Switzerland) but have a genuine and sufficient link to the UK (e.g., working in the UK, habitually resident, significant UK connection)
3. You're a Crown servant or member of HM Forces posted overseas
4. Your partner is a Crown servant or member of HM Forces posted overseas

Which of these situations applies to you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm normally resident here in England - Alex and I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! You normally live in the UK (in Manchester), so you meet the residency requirements.

Now, an important question about your relationship with Alex:

**Are you responsible for Alex?**

This means do any of the following apply:

1. **Alex lives with you** (which you've already mentioned he does), OR
2. You contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount toward Alex's upkeep (even if he doesn't live with you)

Since you mentioned Alex lives at home with you, this should be straightforward. Can you confirm that Alex lives with you and you're responsible for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's right. Alex lives with me at home in Manchester, and I'm fully responsible for him - I provide for all his needs, housing, food, clothes, everything.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! You're clearly responsible for Alex as he lives with you and you provide for all his needs.

Now I need to check:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

This is important because only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child at a time. For example:
- Alex's other parent
- A grandparent
- Another relative or guardian

Are you aware of anyone else who is already claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. It's just me - I'm his primary carer and no one else is getting any benefits for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Since no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, we can continue.

Now I need to confirm:

**Does Alex normally live in the UK?**

You've mentioned he lives with you in Manchester, so this should be yes, but I need to confirm this officially as part of the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Actually, let me ask this more specifically:

**Which of the following best describes Alex's current living situation?**

1. **Living normally at home with you** (in Manchester)
2. **In hospital** for medical treatment
3. **In local authority care** (looked after by the council)
4. **In a foster care arrangement**

Based on what you've told me, it sounds like option 1 - he's living normally at home with you. Is that correct?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, that's correct - option 1. Alex lives normally at home with me here in Manchester. He's not in hospital, care, or fostered - just a regular situation with him living at home.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Alex is living normally at home with you.

Now, one more important question:

**Is the local council (local authority) paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance?**

For example:
- Are they contributing to housing costs for Alex?
- Are they making payments toward his upkeep or care?

This would be different from things like Council Tax reduction or Housing Benefit that might be paid to you as a parent - I'm asking specifically about payments made for Alex's accommodation or maintenance.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. I provide for everything myself - his housing, food, clothes, all his care. There's no council support like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! Based on all the information you've provided, let me navigate to the final outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## 🎉 Great News - You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit!

**Result: ELIGIBLE** ✅

You meet all the Child Benefit eligibility requirements for your son Alex.

### Why You're Eligible:

**For Alex (your child):**
- ✅ He's 8 years old (under 16)
- ✅ Not working full-time
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in his own name
- ✅ Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship
- ✅ Lives normally at home with you in the UK

**For You (the claimant):**
- ✅ You normally live in the UK (Manchester)
- ✅ You're responsible for Alex (he lives with you and you provide for all his needs)
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- ✅ The local council is not paying for Alex's accommodation or maintenance

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and Alex
3. **Provide Alex's birth certificate** or adoption certificate
4. **Be aware of the High Income Child Benefit Charge**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay some or all of the benefit back through tax
5. **Payment schedule**: Payments are typically made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

### How to Apply:

You can find the application form and more information here: **https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim**

Do you have any questions about the next steps or the application process?

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) He is 8 years old (under 16), which qualifies him as a potential qualifying child; (2) He is not working full-time (24+ hours per week); (3) He is not receiving income-based benefits in his own name; (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship - he's in regular primary school; (5) You, as the claimant, meet UK residency requirements as you normally live in Manchester, England; (6) You are responsible for Alex as he lives with you and you provide for all his needs; (7) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex lives normally at home with you in the UK; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria have been met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. Alex meets all the child-related criteria (age under 16, not working full-time, not receiving benefits, not in disqualifying apprenticeship, living normally at home). The claimant meets all the claimant-related criteria (UK residency, responsibility for the child, no other person claiming, council not paying for the child). All eligibility requirements under the UK Child Benefit rules have been satisfied.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements

Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit because: (1) He is 8 years old (under 16), which qualifies him as a potential qualifying child; (2) He is not working full-time (24+ hours per week); (3) He is not receiving income-based benefits in his own name; (4) He is not in a disqualifying apprenticeship - he's in regular primary school; (5) You, as the claimant, meet UK residency requirements as you normally live in Manchester, England; (6) You are responsible for Alex as he lives with you and you provide for all his needs; (7) No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex; (8) Alex lives normally at home with you in the UK; (9) The local council is not paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance. All eligibility criteria have been met.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for their 8-year-old son Alex. Alex meets all the child-related criteria (age under 16, not working full-time, not receiving benefits, not in disqualifying apprenticeship, living normally at home). The claimant meets all the claimant-related criteria (UK residency, responsibility for the child, no other person claiming, council not paying for the child). All eligibility requirements under the UK Child Benefit rules have been satisfied.
